input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML (`ItemsPage.xaml`)**

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:MyApp.ViewModels"
             x:Class="MyApp.Views.ItemsPage"
             x:DataType="vm:ItemsViewModel">

    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
                    IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"
                    RemainingItemsThreshold="0">

        <!-- Pull-to-refresh via RefreshView wrapper -->
        <CollectionView.Behaviors>
            <!-- or wrap in RefreshView instead -->
        </CollectionView.Behaviors>
    </CollectionView>
</ContentPage>
```

In practice, wrap the `CollectionView` in a `RefreshView` for pull-to-refresh:

```xml
<RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
             IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">

    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}">
        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate x:DataType="vm:ItemModel">
                <Grid Padding="12" ColumnDefinitions="*,Auto">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
                    <Label Grid.Column="1" Text="{Binding Status}"
                           TextColor="Gray" />
                </Grid>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>

</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel (`ItemsViewModel.cs`)**

```csharp
public partial class ItemsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;
    private readonly INavigationService _nav;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ObservableCollection<ItemModel> _items = new();

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool _isRefreshing;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ItemModel? _selectedItem;

    // Fires whenever SelectedItem changes
    partial void OnSelectedItemChanged(ItemModel? value)
    {
        if (value is null) return;
        _nav.NavigateAsync("DetailPage", new() { ["Item"] = value });
        SelectedItem = null; // reset so the same row can be tapped again
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task RefreshAsync()
    {
        try
        {
            var data = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
            Items = new ObservableCollection<ItemModel>(data);
        }
        finally
        {
            IsRefreshing = false; // stops the spinner
        }
    }
}
```

**Key bindings explained:**

| Binding | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `ItemsSource → Items` | Drives the list from an `ObservableCollection` |
| `SelectedItem → SelectedItem` | Two-way; the partial method `OnSelectedItemChanged` triggers navigation |
| `IsRefreshing → IsRefreshing` | Two-way; `RefreshView` sets it `true` on pull, you set it `false` when done |
| `Command → RefreshCommand` | Generated by `[RelayCommand]` on `RefreshAsync`; called on pull |

**Important details:**

- **Reset `SelectedItem` to `null`** after navigating — otherwise tapping the same row twice won't fire again.
- **Set `IsRefreshing = false`** in a `finally` block so the spinner always stops, even on errors.
- Uses **CommunityToolkit.Mvvm** (`[ObservableProperty]`, `[RelayCommand]`) to eliminate boilerplate.
